task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OrientDB Studio interface with the 'demodb' database active (usually visible in the top header or database selection screen).

task_completion.description: Agent added a String property named 'Market' to the 'Hotels' class
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Schema manager in OrientDB Studio with the 'Hotels' class selected, displaying a newly created property named 'Market' of type 'STRING', or an ALTER CLASS/CREATE PROPERTY SQL command executed in the console.

task_completion.description: Agent queried the visitor history to compare Profile Nationality with Hotel Country
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show SQL queries in the OrientDB Studio Query/Browse tab that traverse the 'HasStayed' edges, joining 'Profile' and 'Hotels' classes to retrieve or aggregate 'Nationality' and 'Country' fields.

task_completion.description: Agent populated the 'Market' property with 'International' for applicable hotels
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show UPDATE queries setting Market='International' based on the 50% threshold, or the Browse/Graph view showing Hotel records with the 'Market' field set to 'International'.

task_completion.description: Agent populated the 'Market' property with 'Domestic' for applicable hotels
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show UPDATE queries setting Market='Domestic' based on the 50% threshold, or the Browse/Graph view showing Hotel records with the 'Market' field set to 'Domestic'.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the OrientDB Studio GUI in a web browser
integrity.visual_evidence: The actions (queries, schema updates) are performed within the OrientDB web interface rather than exclusively through a terminal script.

integrity.description: Agent actually executed queries to determine the classification rather than guessing
integrity.visual_evidence: The query result panels show actual data being returned from the database before the UPDATE commands are executed.

integrity.description: Agent did not just write the answers into a text file
integrity.visual_evidence: The updates are applied directly to the database records in OrientDB, not just typed out as a list in a text editor or notes app.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires modifying the 'demodb' database. The target class is 'Hotels'. A new property named 'Market' must be added to this class. The valid values for this property are 'International' and 'Domestic'. The classification logic is: 'International' if 50% or more of the incoming 'HasStayed' edges are from Profiles with a Nationality different from the Hotel's Country; otherwise 'Domestic'.
